Sunday, March 22, 2026 Daily Digest

OpenAI Doubles Workforce to 8K, Google Pomelli Goes Global, HubSpot's AI "Intelligence Layer" Arrives

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

OpenAI to Nearly Double Workforce to 8,000 by End-2026

OpenAI is on a hiring tear — planning to grow from 4,500 to 8,000 employees by year's end. The focus: engineering, research, sales, and "technical ambassadors" to help enterprises deploy AI. This is OpenAI betting big on the enterprise market, and the competition for AI talent just got fiercer.

Read on Reuters →
2

Google Pomelli Goes Global: Full Campaigns From Your Website DNA

Google Labs' Pomelli has expanded globally. Give it your URL, and it scans text, images, colors, and fonts to build a "Business DNA" — then generates copy, product photos, social posts, and animated video for complete campaigns. It's free. The commoditization of creative work accelerates.

Read on Mint →
3

Agentforce Hits $540M ARR, 18,500 Enterprise Customers

New data shows Salesforce's Agentforce is the most commercially successful pure-play agentic AI platform with $540 million in annual recurring revenue and 18,500 enterprise customers. The $9 billion enterprise agentic AI market is real — and Salesforce is proving the revenue model works.

Read on Tech Insider →
4

HubSpot's 2026 AI: An "Intelligence Layer" Across All Hubs

HubSpot's latest updates reveal an AI layer that now reads structured data, unstructured data, and behavioral signals across marketing, sales, and service simultaneously. New prospecting tools identify buying signals and suggest who to contact. This is HubSpot's answer to the "siloed AI" problem.

Read on MarTech →
5

2026 MarTech Stack Reality Check: Why Platform Choice Still Matters

Deep dive on the MarTech landscape shows Salesforce Marketing Cloud still dominates enterprise, HubSpot wins the integrated-platform-without-complexity race, and Microsoft Dynamics is quietly eating share. The insight: AI features are table stakes now — the real differentiator is how well they integrate across the stack.

Read on Viewpoint Analysis →

💡 My Take

Watch this one: Google Pomelli. When a free tool can generate entire campaigns from just a URL, the commoditization of creative work accelerates fast. The role shifts from "producing content" to "directing and validating AI-generated content at scale." The winners will be those who build systems around these tools, not compete against them.
